message:
test: baseline snapshot coverage for pills, tabs, role gutters, and find bar (2dccg.6.2)

Add 10 deterministic snapshot tests covering active/inactive pill states,
tab bar rendering, role gutter symbols, find bar open/closed/match states,
and cross-degradation resilience. These form the baseline contract for
downstream regression suites.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.3.4","title":"Profile markdown theming overhead and cache strategy in detail pane","description":"## Goal
Ensure theme-aware markdown rendering does not create noticeable latency or frame jank.

## Work Items
- Measure render-time impact before/after themed renderer wiring
- Identify reuse/caching opportunities if renderer construction is hot
- Document acceptable performance envelope and any trade-offs

## Acceptance
- No visible lag introduced in detail pane navigation and scrolling
- If caching is added, cache invalidation on theme change is correct","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:35:57.005890910Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:25.361978656Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.3.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:35:57.005890910Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.3.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:39:28.613121845Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":354,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.3.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution contract for this task:
1) Treat this bead title+description as the authoritative scope; do not rely on external planning docs.

3) Provide deterministic before/after evidence (tests, snapshots, logs, or captures as applicable).
4) Validate across relevant themes/profiles/breakpoints/degradation levels for this surface.
5) Explicitly document tradeoffs, residual risks, and follow-up opportunities.
6) Do not close until acceptance criteria are demonstrably satisfied with linked evidence artifacts.","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:25Z"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.4","title":"Track D: Detail pane affordance polish (find bar, focus cues, readability)","description":"## Objective
Improve in-pane navigability and reduce visual ambiguity in the right-hand detail panel.

## Why This Exists
The detail find bar currently appears as flat text with no container styling, making active search state easy to miss and reducing confidence in keyboard-driven navigation.

## Scope
- Add visual treatment for find bar (container, contrast, focus signal)
- Ensure match counters/current match are legible across themes
- Verify no overlap with content scroll indicator and tab row

## Deliverables
- Find bar styling that is clearly distinct from content body
- Themed, accessible contrast levels for find query + match state
- Test coverage for render behavior when find bar is open/closed

## Completion Criteria
- Users can instantly distinguish content vs. find UI
- Find bar remains readable in all theme presets and degradation modes
- Existing find keybindings (`/`, `n`, `N`, `Esc`) remain intact","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:33:39.207143773Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:28.295454800Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:33:39.207143773Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:38:30.067444857Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":252,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Track D Intent
Find-in-detail is a high-frequency workflow. Visual ambiguity here disproportionately hurts UX, even if search core logic is correct.

### Quality Bar
The find bar should read like an interactive control, not ambient text. Active query and match counters must remain legible across themes and narrow layouts.","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:41:40Z"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.4.1","title":"Define semantic style tokens and visual spec for detail find bar","description":"## Goal
Specify the find bar visual language using semantic tokens rather than one-off colors.

## Work Items
- Decide token set (e.g., find-bar container, active query, match-count state)
- Define style behavior by theme and degradation level
- Ensure spec includes focus visibility and contrast expectations

## Deliverables
- Token/spec update in style system docs/code comments
- Explicit mapping between find-bar parts and semantic tokens

## Acceptance
- Find bar styling is theme-aware by design
- Future tweaks can be done by token edits without hunting render code","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:36:05.376544012Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:50:27.906456299Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T22:50:27.906433086Z","close_reason":"Completed semantic find-bar token contract + docs mapping + regression checks; renderer wiring explicitly deferred to 2dccg.4.2.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.4.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:56:43.409517297Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.4.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:36:05.376544012Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":355,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.4.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution contract for this task:

6) Do not close until acceptance criteria are demonstrably satisfied with linked evidence artifacts.","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:25Z"},{"id":437,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.4.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"GoldHarbor claim (2026-02-08): implementing semantic find-bar token contract in style_system + docs mapping, with degradation/theme expectations and validation evidence.","created_at":"2026-02-08T22:44:56Z"},{"id":438,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.4.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implementation evidence (GoldHarbor, 2026-02-08):\n\nTouched files:\n- src/ui/style_system.rs\n- docs/tui_style_spec.md\n\nWhat was implemented:\n1) Semantic token contract for detail find bar in style system:\n   - STYLE_DETAIL_FIND_CONTAINER\n   - STYLE_DETAIL_FIND_QUERY\n   - STYLE_DETAIL_FIND_MATCH_ACTIVE\n   - STYLE_DETAIL_FIND_MATCH_INACTIVE\n2) Token derivation added to build_stylesheet() with theme-derived colors only (no hardcoded per-theme literals).\n3) Style-system docs updated with explicit part->token mapping and degradation/theme expectations in docs/tui_style_spec.md (new 'Detail Find Bar Token Contract (2dccg.4.1)' section).\n4) Token registry/tests updated:\n   - Required semantic style list includes new find tokens.\n   - Added tests: detail_find_token_hierarchy_is_explicit_and_theme_aware, detail_find_tokens_remain_legible_in_mono_mode.\n   - Registry/dead-token audit updated with explicit temporary whitelist note that wiring to renderer is owned by 2dccg.4.2.\n\nValidation evidence:\n- cargo test detail_find_token_ -- --nocapture ✅\n- cargo test style_token_registry_is_complete -- --nocapture ✅\n- cargo test no_dead_style_tokens -- --nocapture ✅\n- cargo check --all-targets ✅\n- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings ✅\n\nNote on fmt gate:\n- Global cargo fmt --check is currently failing due unrelated in-flight formatting drift in src/ui/app.rs (reserved by another active agent).\n- This lane avoided touching reserved app.rs regions; style_system/doc changes were kept formatted via cargo fmt on this lane’s edited file(s).\n\nAcceptance mapping:\n- Theme-aware design: yes (tokens derived from resolved semantic palette).\n- Explicit part->token mapping: yes (code + docs section).\n- Focus/contrast expectations: yes (active vs inactive state hierarchy in tokens/tests + docs guidance).\n- Future tweakability by token edits: yes (contract isolated in style_system token definitions).\n\nResidual risk / follow-up:\n- Renderer wiring to consume these new tokens in the detail find bar remains in 2dccg.4.2 by design.","created_at":"2026-02-08T22:50:21Z"}]}
-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.4.2","title":"Implement styled find bar container with clear focus and match-state affordances","description":"## Goal
Replace the current flat find text line with a distinct, high-signal find UI element.

## Work Items
- Render find bar with container/background/border treatment
- Style query text and match count with semantic token roles
- Ensure layout coexists with tab row and scroll indicator at narrow widths
- Preserve existing key interactions and close behavior

## Acceptance
- Users can visually parse find mode instantly
- No clipping/overlap when terminal width is constrained
- Degradation fallback remains readable when decorative styling is reduced","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:36:12.972503556Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T23:18:19.811163976Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.4.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:36:12.972503556Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.4.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:39:35.960586734Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":356,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.4.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution contract for this task:

6) Do not close until acceptance criteria are demonstrably satisfied with linked evidence artifacts.","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:26Z"},{"id":447,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.4.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"AzureElk claim (2026-02-08): implementing styled find bar container.","created_at":"2026-02-08T23:33:56Z"},{"id":448,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.4.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed `coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.4.2` (styled detail find bar container + semantic token wiring).

## What changed
- `src/ui/app.rs`
  - Added `build_detail_find_bar_line(...)` helper to render structured find-bar text with semantic role segmentation:
    - query content
    - active match index (high emphasis)
    - inactive match metadata (secondary emphasis)
    - empty-query hint state
    - deterministic width elision preserving match context
  - Updated `render_detail_pane(...)` find-bar layout logic:
    - active find now reserves a dedicated container region with background treatment
    - uses top-border treatment when space allows (2-row find region)
    - keeps 1-row fallback when height is constrained
    - applies semantic style tokens from `2dccg.4.1`:
      - `STYLE_DETAIL_FIND_CONTAINER`
      - `STYLE_DETAIL_FIND_QUERY`
      - `STYLE_DETAIL_FIND_MATCH_ACTIVE`
      - `STYLE_DETAIL_FIND_MATCH_INACTIVE`
  - Kept existing interaction behavior intact (`/`, `n`, `N`, `Esc`) by only changing render composition.

## Tests added
- `detail_find_bar_line_formats_query_and_match_state`
- `detail_find_bar_line_uses_hint_for_empty_query`
- `detail_find_bar_line_truncates_long_query_but_keeps_match_context`
- `detail_find_bar_render_shows_query_and_match_state_in_detail_pane`
- `detail_find_bar_render_preserves_match_context_on_narrow_widths`
- `detail_find_bar_render_remains_readable_across_degradation_levels`

## Validation evidence
- `cargo test detail_find_bar_line -- --nocapture` ✅
- `cargo test detail_find_bar_render_ -- --nocapture` ✅
- `cargo test detail_find_ -- --nocapture` ✅
- `cargo test tui_pty_search_detail_and_quit_flow --test e2e_tui_smoke_flows -- --nocapture` ✅
- `cargo check --all-targets` ✅
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` ✅
- `cargo fmt --check` ✅

## Acceptance mapping
- Distinct, high-signal find UI: ✅
  - dedicated container background + border treatment in detail pane.
- Query/match state semantic styling: ✅
  - explicit token wiring for query, active match, inactive metadata.
- Narrow-width coexistence: ✅
  - constrained-width rendering keeps match context and avoids overlap/clipping.
- Key interactions preserved: ✅
  - behavior unchanged; render-only modifications.
- Degradation readability: ✅
  - dedicated regression test across `Full`, `SimpleBorders`, `NoStyling`, `EssentialOnly`.

## Tradeoffs / residual risk
- No dedicated border token exists for find bar; border treatment currently reuses pane focus/unfocus border styles for coherence.
- Full visual snapshot baselines for this surface are still primarily covered by downstream snapshot lane (`coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6.2` / `2dccg.4.3`), while this lane adds deterministic logic/render tests.
","created_at":"2026-02-08T23:34:24Z"},{"id":449,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.4.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"AzureElk verification (2026-02-08): Bead already fully implemented by prior work.

## Evidence
- All 4 style tokens (CONTAINER, QUERY, MATCH_ACTIVE, MATCH_INACTIVE) defined in style_system.rs:1540-1559
- All 4 tokens wired into rendering at app.rs:5296-5350
- Container rendering: Block with TOP border (2-line mode) or plain Block (1-line fallback)
- Line composition: build_detail_find_bar_line() at app.rs:2666-2733 with per-span styling
- 15 tests pass: toggle, navigation, highlighting, narrow-width preservation, degradation levels
- Acceptance met: find mode visually distinct, no clipping at narrow widths, degradation readable","created_at":"2026-02-08T23:35:44Z"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.4.3","title":"Add render/interaction tests for detail find bar visual states","description":"## Goal
Ensure find bar polish remains stable under future refactors.

## Scope Boundary (Canonical)
This bead owns **detail-find-bar component-level render/interaction regression checks**.
- Includes: visible find-bar states and local find-bar interaction outcomes in the detail pane.
- Excludes: global event-routing/state-machine ownership (`2dccg.11.2`) and broader cross-surface rendering matrix ownership (`2dccg.11.1`).

## Work Items
- Add tests for closed/open/no-matches/has-matches/current-match states.
- Add snapshot or line-structure assertions for styled find bar output.
- Verify `Esc` closes find bar without closing detail modal.
- Add focused assertions for match-counter formatting consistency across presets.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Existing behavior tests pass and new visual-state tests catch regressions.
- Match counter behavior remains correct after styling changes.
- Suite stays narrowly scoped to detail find bar outcomes (no cross-surface duplication).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:36:20.043890205Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:05:14.327208462Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.4.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:36:20.043890205Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.4.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:39:36.251889021Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":357,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.4.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution contract for this task:

5) Explicitly document tradeoffs, residual risks, and follow-up opportunities.
6) Do not close until acceptance criteria are demonstrably satisfied with linked evidence artifacts.","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:26Z"},{"id":420,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.4.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Planning-space optimization pass (2026-02-08): tightened canonical scope boundaries to remove test ownership overlap while preserving full feature/test intent. Explicit include/exclude contracts now separate component-level, surface-level, policy-matrix, and cross-cutting verification responsibilities so parallel execution is safer and less duplicative.","created_at":"2026-02-08T22:04:09Z"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5","title":"Track E: Recalibrate degradation policy to preserve essential styling","description":"## Objective
Tune degradation behavior so terminals with sufficient capability keep meaningful styling rather than prematurely collapsing into bland output.

## Why This Exists
A suspected contributor to the quality drop is over-aggressive degradation logic tied to frame budget/terminal capability that strips colors/backgrounds too early.

## Scope
- Audit degradation decisions in render pipeline
- Define minimum visual hierarchy that should survive moderate degradation
- Adjust thresholds/policy and validate against representative terminal profiles

## Deliverables
- Documented degradation policy table (capability -> retained style layers)
- Updated heuristics/thresholds or guards in code
- Regression checks for no-style / low-style / full-style scenarios

## Completion Criteria
- Capable terminals retain tabs/pills/role hierarchy consistently
- Low-capability terminals remain readable without broken contrast
- Behavior is deterministic and testable (not ad-hoc per frame)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:33:47.279389078Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:28.544781336Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:33:47.279389078Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:38:30.698611175Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":253,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Track E Intent
FrankenTUI’s degradation system is powerful, but if tuned too aggressively it can erase the very hierarchy we worked to build.

### Strategy
Audit first, then tune. Avoid blind threshold changes. Preserve readability and hierarchy while keeping low-capability terminals functional.","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:41:40Z"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.1","title":"Audit degradation decision flow and identify style layers stripped per level","description":"## Goal
Make degradation behavior explicit and inspectable.

## Work Items
- Trace degradation inputs (terminal capabilities, frame budget, runtime heuristics)
- Enumerate which style layers are disabled at each degradation stage
- Produce a compact matrix: degradation level -> retained UI affordances

## Deliverables
- Auditable report attached to bead comments/docs
- Candidate list of over-aggressive strips affecting pills/tabs/gutters/markdown

## Acceptance
- Team can point to exact logic responsible for visual flattening
- Findings are specific enough to guide policy adjustments in follow-up tasks","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:36:29.152141848Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:10:16.179984071Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T22:10:16.179961128Z","close_reason":"Completed degradation-flow audit with code-path mapping, per-level strip matrix, and follow-up candidate list documented in bead comments.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:56:39.253370420Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:36:29.152141848Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":358,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution contract for this task:
1) Treat this bead title+description as the authoritative scope; do not rely on external planning docs.
2) Record touched files/functions and behavior surfaces in completion notes.

4) Validate across relevant themes/profiles/breakpoints/degradation levels for this surface.
5) Explicitly document tradeoffs, residual risks, and follow-up opportunities.
6) Do not close until acceptance criteria are demonstrably satisfied with linked evidence artifacts.","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:26Z"},{"id":429,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Audit findings (2026-02-08) — degradation decision flow and stripped-style matrix

Code-path map
1. Runtime budget profile is configured in `src/ui/app.rs:12753-12760` using `FrameBudgetConfig { total: 120ms, allow_frame_skip: false, degradation_cooldown: 12, upgrade_threshold: 0.25, ..relaxed() }`.
2. Render-time degradation gates are read in `src/ui/app.rs:11615-11637` (`frame.degradation`) and projected into four booleans: `use_unicode_borders()`, `render_decorative()`, `apply_styling()`, `render_content()`.
3. Those gates directly control:
   - border type fallback (`Rounded` -> `Square`) at `src/ui/app.rs:11619-11623`
   - whether pane/modal borders render (`Borders::ALL` vs `Borders::NONE`) at `src/ui/app.rs:11626-11630`
   - whether semantic styles are applied vs plain monochrome at `src/ui/app.rs:11636-11647` and related style slots
   - whether search query inset styling is shown at `src/ui/app.rs:11804-11815`
   - whether pane content body is rendered (analytics/sources/modal content paths) at `src/ui/app.rs:12138-12358`
4. Degradation-level semantics come from ftui in `ftui-render/src/budget.rs:621-730`:
   - `Full`: unicode borders + styling + decorative + content
   - `SimpleBorders`: unicode off, styling/decor/content still on
   - `NoStyling`: unicode off, styling off, decor/content on
   - `EssentialOnly`: unicode off, styling off, decorative off, content on
   - `Skeleton`: content off (layout shells only)
   - `SkipFrame`: max degradation

Observed stripping matrix (current behavior)
- `Full`: no strip
- `SimpleBorders`: rounded/box-drawing affordance downgraded to square/ascii-like look
- `NoStyling`: all semantic color/attribute hierarchy collapses to plain style (chips/tabs/gutters cues flatten)
- `EssentialOnly`: borders/decorative chrome removed (`Borders::NONE`) while content remains
- `Skeleton`: content text suppressed in gated paths; pane shells persist
- `SkipFrame`: max level; in this app frame-skip is disabled (`allow_frame_skip=false`), but visual output remains at effectively skeleton/no-content behavior where gates apply

Cross-check with tests
- Enum progression + gate semantics validated in `src/ui/app.rs:16452-16479`.
- Border suppression expectation validated in `src/ui/app.rs:17129-17152`.

Candidate over-aggressive flattening contributors for follow-up (`2dccg.5.2` / `2dccg.10.6`)
- Hard style collapse at `NoStyling` removes semantic differentiation too early for capable terminals under transient pressure.
- `render_decorative()` gate currently disables all borders at `EssentialOnly`, which can reduce scannability in dense panes.
- Decision thresholds for controller/e-process are largely inherited from ftui defaults (`ftui-render/src/budget.rs:342-357`, `454-481`) and should be revalidated against cass workload characteristics.
","created_at":"2026-02-08T22:08:48Z"}]}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.4","title":"Expose degradation state in diagnostics/status for easier field triage","description":"## Goal
Make active degradation state visible so bug reports can include concrete runtime context.

## Work Items
- Ensure status/footer diagnostics clearly show current degradation mode
- Add optional debug detail for why a mode was selected (capability vs perf)
- Document how to capture this in issue reports

## Acceptance
- Agents can quickly determine whether visual flatness is due to degradation mode
- Troubleshooting no longer requires deep code spelunking for basic state info","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:36:52.039824793Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T20:42:01.574187601Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T20:42:01.574155942Z","close_reason":"Superseded and merged into canonical diagnostics bead 2dccg.10.9 to avoid overlap while preserving scope.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:36:52.039824793Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:39:46.478017233Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6","title":"Track F: Rich fixtures + snapshot coverage for visual regression protection","description":"## Objective
Upgrade test fixtures and snapshot coverage so the UI quality we care about is actually exercised by automated tests.

## Why This Exists
Current snapshots use minimal synthetic content that under-represents real-world visual complexity, allowing regressions in styling, hierarchy, and affordances to slip through.

## Scope
- Create richer fixture data (multi-agent roles, tool calls, markdown/code blocks, long snippets, score spread)
- Add snapshot variants covering pills, tabs, detail view, and role gutters
- Ensure tests are deterministic and cheap enough for regular local/CI runs

## Deliverables
- Expanded fixture builders/data helpers
- New snapshot baselines for key visual surfaces
- Test docs clarifying how to regenerate snapshots when intentional UI changes happen

## Completion Criteria
- Visual regressions in key surfaces produce obvious snapshot diffs
- Fixtures represent realistic cass workloads (not toy text-only lines)
- Snapshot updates are deliberate and reviewable","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:33:56.403007457Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:28.796092040Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:33:56.403007457Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:38:30.594645265Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":254,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Track F Intent
If tests do not model realistic UI complexity, regressions will keep recurring.

### Testing Philosophy
Prefer smaller targeted snapshots over giant omnibus snapshots. Keep fixtures rich, deterministic, and purpose-specific so diffs are explainable.","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:41:40Z"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6.1","title":"Build high-fidelity fixture dataset for TUI visual scenarios","description":"## Goal
Create fixture data that mirrors real cass usage patterns and stresses the UI meaningfully.

## Work Items
- Include mixed agent roles (user/assistant/tool/system/other)
- Include markdown-heavy messages with code blocks, headings, lists, links
- Include long snippets, varied score distributions, and source/host metadata
- Include enough rows to exercise list striping/selection/scroll indicators

## Acceptance
- Fixture reveals styling regressions that minimal synthetic data misses
- Dataset remains deterministic and reusable across tests","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:37:00.810533151Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:23:04.286292505Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T22:23:04.286266016Z","close_reason":"Completed: deterministic high-fidelity TUI fixture dataset + invariants + render stress coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:39:59.260259733Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:37:00.810533151Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":361,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution contract for this task:
1) Treat this bead title+description as the authoritative scope; do not rely on external planning docs.
2) Record touched files/functions and behavior surfaces in completion notes.
3) Provide deterministic before/after evidence (tests, snapshots, logs, or captures as applicable).
4) Validate across relevant themes/profiles/breakpoints/degradation levels for this surface.
5) Explicitly document tradeoffs, residual risks, and follow-up opportunities.
6) Do not close until acceptance criteria are demonstrably satisfied with linked evidence artifacts.","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:28Z"},{"id":431,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution summary (CyanPuma, 2026-02-08 UTC):

Touched files / behavior surfaces
- `src/ui/app.rs:16537` added `rich_visual_fixture_hits()` as a deterministic high-fidelity TUI corpus with:
  - mixed agent/source provenance
  - markdown-heavy content (headings, lists, links, code blocks, JSON/table/text blocks)
  - long snippets for truncation/wrap stress
  - varied score distribution + source/host metadata for hierarchy stress
- `src/ui/app.rs:16695` added `app_with_rich_visual_fixture()` reusable app builder for rendering tests.
- `src/ui/app.rs:16721` added `rich_visual_fixture_dataset_is_deterministic_and_diverse()` to enforce fixture determinism + richness invariants.
- `src/ui/app.rs:16768` added `rich_visual_fixture_renders_across_layouts_without_panic()` to exercise fixture-backed rendering across narrow/medium/wide layouts.

Acceptance mapping
- Mixed scenario richness: enforced by invariant test checks (agent diversity, markdown/code presence, long snippet presence, remote host metadata, wide score range).
- Deterministic + reusable: fixture is generated by one helper (`rich_visual_fixture_hits`) and consumed by multiple tests via `app_with_rich_visual_fixture`.
- Rendering stress usefulness: no-panic render coverage now runs the richer corpus at multiple breakpoints.

Validation run
- `cargo test rich_visual_fixture_dataset_is_deterministic_and_diverse -- --nocapture`
- `cargo test rich_visual_fixture_renders_across_layouts_without_panic -- --nocapture`
- `cargo check --all-targets`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --check`

Tradeoffs / residual risk
- This bead establishes the canonical dataset + invariant checks; full snapshot expansion consuming this corpus is intentionally left to follow-on snapshot owners (`2dccg.6.2`, `2dccg.8.6`, `2dccg.9.5`, `2dccg.11.3`).
- Role diversity is represented through agent/source-rich payloads in `SearchHit` (the available fixture type on this surface) rather than a separate role field.
","created_at":"2026-02-08T22:22:53Z"}]}
-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6.2","title":"Add snapshot coverage for pills, tabs, role gutters, and detail find states","description":"## Goal
Create the shared baseline snapshot layer for core cross-surface affordances implicated in the ftui quality regression.

## Scope Boundary (Canonical)
This bead is the **foundational snapshot contract** used by Track H/I/K regression suites.
- Includes: minimal, purpose-specific snapshots for pills/tabs/role gutters/find states.
- Excludes: richer search-surface behavior suites (Track H `2dccg.8.6`) and results-surface richness/perf suites (Track I `2dccg.9.5`), which should extend this baseline rather than re-implement it.

## Work Items
- Add focused deterministic snapshots for:
  - Active/inactive filter pills
  - Detail tab bar active vs inactive
  - Role gutter variation across roles
  - Find bar open/closed/no-match/current-match
- Keep each snapshot narrowly scoped and diff-readable.
- Publish reusable fixture slices/helpers that downstream suites (`2dccg.8.6`, `2dccg.9.5`, `2dccg.11.1`, `2dccg.11.3`) can import.

## Acceptance
- Affordance-level regressions fail with small, actionable diffs.
- Downstream suites reuse baseline fixtures/helpers instead of duplicating equivalent snapshot setups.
- Snapshots remain deterministic under fixed fixtures and terminal parameters.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:37:08.815077754Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T20:46:51.890079217Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:55:06.279015700Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:40:00.496966815Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:40:00.993331446Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:40:11.460627611Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:40:11.520748515Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:40:11.983956610Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:37:08.815077754Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:39:59.583732673Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2kz6t","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:40:00.740053508Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":" ## Files touched
- src/ui/app.rs: Added 10 snapshot tests covering pills, tabs, role gutters, and find bar

## Snapshot coverage
### Pills (3 tests)
- snapshot_pills_active_vs_inactive: verifies inactive placeholder pills + active filter value rendering
- snapshot_pills_deterministic: repeated renders produce identical output
- snapshot_pills_across_degradation: pill content survives Full/SimpleBorders/NoStyling/EssentialOnly

### Tabs (2 tests)
- snapshot_tab_bar_active_inactive: Messages and Raw tab labels visible, switches correctly
- snapshot_tab_bar_deterministic: repeated renders produce identical output

### Role gutters (1 test)
- snapshot_role_gutters_vary_across_roles: User/Agent/Tool messages produce role prefix symbols in rendered output

### Find bar (4 tests)
- snapshot_find_bar_open_with_matches: query text and match position (1/3) visible
- snapshot_find_bar_no_matches: zero state (0/0 or 'no match') shown
- snapshot_find_bar_closed: no match counts when find disabled
- snapshot_find_bar_across_degradation: find bar query survives all degradation levels

## Reusable fixtures
- Tests use existing make_test_conversation_view(), app_with_hits(), app_with_rich_visual_fixture()
- All tests use render_at_degradation + buffer_to_text pattern for deterministic assertions
- Downstream suites (2dccg.8.6, 2dccg.9.5) can extend these patterns

## Validation
- clippy clean, fmt clean
- All 10 new tests pass; no regressions in existing test suite","created_at":"2026-02-08T23:42:02Z"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6.3","title":"Add targeted cross-theme and cross-degradation visual regression matrix","description":"## Goal
Ensure visual fixes hold beyond a single theme/capability profile by executing a compact but high-value cross-theme/degradation matrix.

## Scope Boundary (Canonical)
This bead composes matrix coverage from:
- Baseline affordance snapshots (`2dccg.6.2`)
- Search-surface regression suite (`2dccg.8.6`)
- Results-surface regression suite (`2dccg.9.5`)
It is the integrator layer, not a duplicate suite.

## Work Items
- Select compact matrix: key scenario x representative theme x representative degradation tier.
- Snapshot expected hierarchy retention for each matrix cell.
- Keep matrix intentionally small to control CI runtime while preserving defect-detection value.

## Acceptance
- Regressions that only appear in light/high-contrast or degraded mode are caught.
- Matrix remains maintainable and fast enough for routine runs.
- Matrix scenarios trace back to canonical baseline/extension suites rather than duplicated one-off cases.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:37:16.364483046Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T21:30:50.893816939Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:40:19.132977284Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:40:12.288248896Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:58:38.778101369Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:37:16.364483046Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:40:12.300017409Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.8.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:46:17.285822385Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.9.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:55:06.073002282Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":363,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution contract for this task:

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6.4","title":"Document snapshot regeneration and review protocol for intentional UI changes","description":"## Goal
Avoid snapshot churn and accidental approval of regressions by standardizing regeneration/review workflow.

## Work Items
- Document exact commands for updating snapshots
- Define reviewer checklist for distinguishing intended polish vs unintended regression
- Link protocol from TESTING.md or relevant contributor docs

## Acceptance
- Contributors know when and how to refresh snapshots responsibly
- Snapshot PRs include clear rationale tied to bead IDs and visual goals","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:37:23.900459778Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:37.577464531Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:37:23.900459778Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:40:12.004812253Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":381,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Track orchestration note:
- This bead is a coordination container. Prefer executing ready leaf tasks under this track.
- Keep scope boundaries explicit to avoid duplicate implementation across sibling tracks.

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.7","title":"Track G: README/screenshots/install-script unpin + release readiness","description":"## Objective
Complete the outward-facing release transition from old ratatui-pinned docs/install defaults to polished ftui defaults once visual parity is proven.

## Why This Exists
README and installer messaging currently indicate `v0.1.64` pinning (last ratatui build), which undermines adoption of the new architecture and reflects unresolved UX debt.

## Scope
- Refresh screenshots to represent improved ftui UI
- Update README narrative and release gate language
- Remove default version pin in install scripts when quality gates pass
- Run full quality gates and capture go/no-go evidence

## Deliverables
- Updated `README.md` screenshots and explanatory text
- Updated `install.sh` and `install.ps1` behavior/docs
- Final validation report (fmt/check/clippy/tests/smoke) attached to bead comments

## Completion Criteria
- New users install and see improved ftui build by default
- README visuals match current product behavior

This is the release-control layer that prevents docs/install drift and premature default unpinning.

### Guardrail
Installer unpin is contingent on evidence from parity scoring + regression tests + quality gates, not on schedule pressure.

### Handoff Outcome
By completion, a future agent should be able to audit go/no-go rationale from bead history alone.","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:41:41Z"},{"id":271,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Release Gate Strengthening

Track G now depends on Track K evidence outputs to prevent premature installer/docs changes.

Key gate dependency:
- `2dccg.7.4` and downstream release tasks require `2dccg.11.8` (verification evidence bundle).

This enforces a strict policy: no default unpin or release messaging change without comprehensive unit + E2E + logging-backed validation.","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:57:48Z"}]}
